candidates.why: Separate operating D&A-type summary row above operating profit, but it is generic and not specifically attributed to COGS/cost of sales. Also includes impairment, which is out of scope for this task.
Separate operating depreciation row above operating profit, but no indication it belongs to the selected COGS bucket ('Maintenance expenses') rather than SG&A or company-wide overhead.
Separate operating amortization row above operating profit, but no indication it belongs to the selected COGS bucket. More likely corporate/overhead or mixed.

variables.notes: There are explicit operating D&A rows, but none are labelled as COGS/cost of sales/COR or as components of the selected COGS row 'Maintenance expenses'. To avoid misallocation and double counting, no COGS-specific D&A row is added.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A-type summary row above operating profit and outside the selected SG&A component rows, but it is generic and includes impairment, so it is not cleanly attributable to SG&A.
Separate operating depreciation row above operating profit and outside the selected SG&A component rows, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/selling-related.
Separate operating amortization row above operating profit and outside the selected SG&A component rows, but not labelled as SG&A or R&D. No explicit 'Amortization in R&D' row is present.

variables.notes: MANUALLY CHANGED. Although D&A is disclosed separately in the operating section, the rows are generic and not clearly attributable to the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket ('Administrative Expenses', 'Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses', 'Other operating expenses'). Because XRD was mapped to a supplemental row below operating profit and no SG&A/R&D-specific D&A row is visible, the conservative choice is not to add any XSGA_DA row.
